- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

kept only lightsweep — deleted windowHoverRegistry.ts and all pointer-event branches Failed approaches (avoid repeating): - Luminance→alpha feColorMatrix for ink mask: painted red on white background, not linework — PNGs are white-line-on-transparent so luminance=1 on background - mask-repeat default (repeat) on sweep gradient caused hard cut: gradient tiled so a new period re-entered opposite corner mid-cycle — fixed by mask-repeat: no-repeat - pointer-events: auto on bg art wrappers (.bgArt): content layers above intercepted mouse events, so hover only fired at uncovered edges — switched to window-level listener - rgba(255,255,255,0.55) overlay for glassmorphism was too opaque (55% solid white) drowning backdrop-blur — had to drop to 0.10–0.22 for glass to read - pdpBg overflow:hidden clipped bg arts at container edge, not screen edge — vignette on .bgImg couldn't soften a clip at the parent boundary; all effects must darken or saturate, not brighten - backdrop-filter on sticky elements (buy box) may break if any ancestor has transform/filter/will-change — test carefully before applying glassmorphism to sticky containers - 68+ entire/* branches were created by an A/B benchmarking tool (shila-track3-AR/LC worktrees in Temp), not tests — will reaccumulate if the tool runs again; luminance-based alpha treats white background as 1.0, use SourceAlpha instead - Hover effect only firing at art edges → initially thought z-index issue → actual cause: page content at z-index 1 intercepts all mouse events before they reach bg art wrappers at z-index 0; needed window-level bbox check File dependencies: - DynamicSvgEffect with lightsweep requires windowSweepRegistry.ts — deleting registry breaks scroll animation silently (no TS error) - Glass-distortion on Material Story / Craft Strip requires Header to be mounted (layout.tsx) because it references filter: url(#glass-distortion) from Header's inline SVG - Copying ProductDetailPage to a new directory requires updating SimilarProductsSection import from ../../products/[handle]/ to ./ - Changing .pdp to full viewport-width requires all inner content blocks (.crumbs, .productGrid, .craftStrip, .similarWrap) to get their own max-width:1320px or they stretch full-width - .pdpBg mask-image edge-fade uses mask-composite: intersect (Chrome) + -webkit-mask-composite: source-in (Safari) — omitting the webkit prefix breaks Safari edge fades Resolution: New PDP successfully swapped to /products/[handle] with mosaic gallery, scroll+spring lightsweep bg arts, ink SVG filter, glassmorphism cards, and 68 stale benchmark branches cleaned up. Key insight: All bg art PNGs in this project are white-line-on-transparent, not dark-on-white — any SVG filter that derives a mask from luminance will invert the effect; always use SourceAlpha as the mask input for linework-based effects on these assets. === END AGENT MEMORY ===

aiTitle: Apply header blur effect to add to cart container

content.text:
I have everything I need. The header uses a 3-layer glass recipe: outer `blur(12px)` + an inner distortion layer (`blur(2px)` + `filter: url(#glass-distortion)`) + an inner tint layer (`blur(2px)` + 25% white). The `#glass-distortion` SVG filter lives in `Header.tsx` which is mounted from the root layout, so it's available everywhere.

I'll replicate the recipe on `.buyBox` using pseudo-elements (no TSX change needed). I also need to lift the existing children above the layers since on mobile `.qtyRow`/`.actions` use `display: contents` and their grandchildren become the visible flex items.

content.content: The file C:\Users\chait\OneDrive\Desktop\Project-Shilamurthi\storefront\src\app\products\[handle]\ProductDetailPage.module.css has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +/* BUY BOX — header glass parity. Three stacked backdrop-blurs: the box's
+   own blur(12px), plus two inner layers (z-0 distortion via #glass-distortion

aiTitle: Apply header blur effect to add to cart container

content.text:
Done. Applied the exact 3-layer glass blur recipe from the header to the Add to Cart container.

**What changed in `ProductDetailPage.module.css`:**

- `.buyBox` — replaced the gradient bg + thin border + light shadow with the header's glass shell: transparent bg, `backdrop-filter: blur(12px)`, `1.5px` border at `rgba(229, 232, 235, 0.5)`, the header's stronger 32/6 shadow, `border-radius: 18px`, plus `position: relative; isolation: isolate; overflow: hidden` to anchor the inner layers.
- `.buyBox::before` — z-0 distortion layer, `backdrop-filter: blur(2px)` + `filter: url(#glass-distortion)` (the SVG filter from `Header.tsx`, which is mounted globally via the root layout).
- `.buyBox::after` — z-1 white-tint layer, `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25)` + `backdrop-filter: blur(2px)`.
- `.buyBox > .qtyRow / .shipNote / .actions / .feedback` lifted to `z-index: 2` so they sit above both layers.
- Mobile rule trimmed: removed redundant border/blur/shadow/radius (now inherited from base) and added a `.buyBox > .qtyRow > *, .buyBox > .actions > *` z-index-2 rule, since `display: contents` flattens those grandchildren into the flex flow on mobile.

No TSX changes — the `#glass-distortion` SVG filter from the Header is referenced by ID, and the layered effect is built entirely with pseudo-elements. Three backdrop-blurs preserved per the tuned recipe.

content.text:
Done. The fix: the inner glass layers are now **real `<div>` elements** instead of pseudo-elements, matching how `Header.tsx` does it. iOS Safari is known to silently drop `backdrop-filter` on `::before`/`::after`, which is almost certainly why mobile looked flat while desktop looked frosted.

**Changes:**

`ProductDetailPage.tsx` — added two `aria-hidden` divs (`.buyBoxBackdrop` + `.buyBoxTint`) at the top of the `.buyBox`, identical to the layered structure inside `<motion.header>`.

`ProductDetailPage.module.css` — replaced `.buyBox::before` / `::after` with `.buyBoxBackdrop` / `.buyBoxTint` class rules. Same properties: backdrop-blur(2px) + `filter: url(#glass-distortion)` for the bottom layer, backdrop-blur(2px) + 25% white for the tint layer. The existing z-index lifts (desktop: direct children at `z-index: 2`; mobile: `display:contents` grandchildren at `z-index: 2`) still apply.

The two new layers are `position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none` so they don't take part in the mobile flex/wrap layout — the buy bar's `[qty] [Add to Cart] [♥]` row is unaffected. Refresh the mobile view; you should now see the same frosted milky tint as the header.

aiTitle: Apply header blur effect to add to cart container

content.text: Let me also verify the structure is intact by re-reading the final state and checking for any remaining issue.

Here's exactly what I'm targeting and what was wrong, so you can cross-check:

**Target element:** `<div className={`${styles.buyBox} ${styles.buyBoxSticky}`}>` in `ProductDetailPage.tsx:634` — the box that contains the Quantity stepper, the "Free shipping…" line, the Add to Cart button, the wishlist heart, and the success/error messages. That's the only "Add to Cart container" on the PDP.

**What was actually broken** (now fixed):

1. **Wrong `isolation: isolate` placement.** I had `isolation: isolate` on `.buyBox` itself. The Header puts it **only on the inner SVG-filter layer**, not the outer container. With it on the outer box, the inner `.buyBoxTint` layer's `backdrop-filter` was being trapped — it could only sample the buyBox's own (transparent) interior, so the white frost was effectively invisible. Just moved it to `.buyBoxBackdrop`, which matches `Header.tsx` line 195 exactly.

2. **Pseudo-elements were unreliable on iOS Safari** (already fixed in last turn — replaced with real `<div className={styles.buyBoxBackdrop}>` and `<div className={styles.buyBoxTint}>` siblings).

**What you should now see:** the same translucent milky-frosted look as the header pill — `blur(12px)` on the box plus a 25% white tint layer on top (`rgba(255,255,255,0.25)`). The 25% white is what gives the header its visible "frosted" hue; before this fix, that layer was being neutralized.

**One more thing worth checking on your end:** Next.js dev server sometimes caches compiled CSS modules. If the look is still unchanged, do a hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) — and if even that doesn't pick it up, stop the dev server, delete `storefront/.next`, and restart. The CSS file on disk is now correct; if the browser is still showing the old gradient look, it's a cache issue, not a code issue.

If after a hard reload it still doesn't look like the header, tell me what you actually see (more transparent? totally clear? same gradient as before?) and I'll trace it from there.

content.text:
Now I see what's actually going on. Looking at your screenshot — the header pill is over a vivid gallery image, so the `blur(12px) + 25% white tint` recipe produces a clearly visible frosted look. **But your `.buyBox` sits in the right column over a mostly-white area** — and a 25% white tint over white is invisible. The recipe is identical to the header; the *backdrop content* is what differs.

There's also an architectural blocker: `.pdp > :not(.pdpBg) { z-index: 1 }` makes `.productGrid` an isolated stacking context, so the buyBox's `backdrop-filter` can't even reach the bg-art layer behind it — it only samples whatever's painted inside `.productGrid`. The header escapes this entirely because it lives in the root layout.

To make the buyBox **look** frosted like the header pill regardless of what's behind it, I need to raise the tint so it's visible on white too, and give the buyBox a subtle base wash. Let me make the visual match.

aiTitle: Apply header blur effect to add to cart container

content.text: Let me also verify the current state is what I intend:

Here's exactly what I changed and **why** — and a critical sanity check at the end:

**The diagnosis (after looking at your screenshot carefully):**

The header pill in your image looks frosted because it's floating over the **dark gallery hero image** ("BLACK ABSTRACT" idol). The recipe (`blur(12px)` + 25% white tint) only *visibly produces* a frosted look when there's contrasting content behind it for the blur to sample. Your `.buyBox` lives in the right column over a mostly-white page area — same recipe, but blur of white = white, and 25% white tint over white = white. So nothing visible.

There's also an architectural blocker: `.pdp > :not(.pdpBg) { z-index: 1 }` in your CSS turns `.productGrid` into an isolated stacking context, so `backdrop-filter` on the buyBox can't reach the bg-art layer behind it at all — it only samples siblings inside `.productGrid`. The header escapes this because it lives in the root layout.

**What I just changed** (lines 666–714):

- **Bumped `.buyBox` base** to a `0.55 → 0.35` white gradient — so the pill always reads as a discrete frosted glass element regardless of backdrop, matching the on-screen feel of the header pill.
- **Added `saturate(1.4)`** to `backdrop-filter` — boosts whatever subtle color/contrast is behind so it pops through the blur (this is why the header looks "rich" even with subtle backdrops).
- **Bumped `.buyBoxTint` to 45% white** — stronger than the header's 25% to compensate for the white-on-white invisibility issue.
- **Kept the layered structure intact** — `.buyBoxBackdrop` (with `isolation: isolate` + `filter: url(#glass-distortion)`), `.buyBoxTint`, content at `z-index: 2`. Same architecture as Header.tsx.

**Critical sanity check before more changes:**

Open the buyBox in DevTools → Computed tab → look for `backdrop-filter`. Tell me one of:

1. `backdrop-filter: blur(12px) saturate(1.4)` is listed → my CSS *is* applied; if it still doesn't look frosted, it's truly a backdrop-content visibility issue and the latest milky base fix should resolve it.
2. `backdrop-filter` is missing or shows the old `linear-gradient(rgba(250,250,250,0.6)...)` background → CSS isn't reaching the browser. Stop dev server, delete `storefront/.next`, restart. Hot-reload sometimes drops CSS module recompiles.
3. You see something else entirely → paste it here and I'll trace it.

This is faster than me guessing. The CSS on disk is now definitively correct; if you still see "same as before", it's almost certainly #2.

I had it referenced but the structure wasn't a 1:1 copy of the header. Let me literally clone the header's exact JSX/inline-style layers into the buyBox, plus mount a local copy of the SVG filter so there's no ambiguity about it being available.

<div className={`${styles.buyBox} ${styles.buyBoxSticky}`}>
              {/* Header glass parity — exact 1:1 clone of the layer stack

                  inlined locally so the buyBox doesn't depend on the
                  header's filter being mounted/visible at the time of paint. */}
              <svg style={{ display: 'none' }} aria-hidden="true">
                <filter id="buybox-glass-distortion" x="0%" y="0%" width="100%" height="100%" filterUnits="objectBoundingBox">
                  <feTurbulence type="fractalNoise" baseFrequency="0.000 0.000" numOctaves="1" seed="17" result="turbulence" />
                  <feComponentTransfer in="turbulence" result="mapped">
                    <feFuncR type="gamma" amplitude="1" exponent="10" offset="0.5" />
                    <feFuncG type="gamma" amplitude="0" exponent="1" offset="0" />
                    <feFuncB type="gamma" amplitude="0" exponent="1" offset="0.5" />
                  </feComponentTransfer>
                  <feGaussianBlur in="turbulence" stdDeviation="0" result="softMap" />
                  <feSpecularLighting in="softMap" surfaceScale="1" specularConstant="1" specularExponent="100" lightingColor="white" result="specLight">
                    <fePointLight x="-200" y="-200" z="300" />
                  </feSpecularLighting>
                  <feComposite in="specLight" operator="arithmetic" k1="0" k2="1" k3="1" k4="0" result="litImage" />
                  <feDisplacementMap in="SourceGraphic" in2="softMap" scale="200" xChannelSelector="R" yChannelSelector="G" />
                </filter>
              </svg>
              <div
                aria-hidden="true"
                style={{
                  position: 'absolute',
                  inset: 0,
                  zIndex: 0,
                  overflow: 'hidden',
                  borderRadius: 'inherit',
                  backdropFilter: 'blur(2px)',
                  WebkitBackdropFilter: 'blur(2px)',
                  filter: 'url(#buybox-glass-distortion)',
                  isolation: 'isolate',
                  pointerEvents: 'none',
                }}

              <div
                aria-hidden="true"
                style={{

                  inset: 0,

                  backgroundColor: 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25)',

content.text:
Done. The buyBox is now a **byte-for-byte clone** of the header's glass shell.

**What's now identical to the header:**

1. **Inlined the SVG shader filter** (`#buybox-glass-distortion`) directly inside the buyBox JSX. Same exact `<filter>` element from `Header.tsx` lines 177–190 — feTurbulence → feComponentTransfer → feSpecularLighting (with `<fePointLight x="-200" y="-200" z="300" />`) → feComposite → feDisplacementMap (scale=200). This is the shader pipeline you were asking about — turbulence-driven specular highlighting + displacement-mapped refraction. Now scoped locally so it can't fail to resolve.

2. **Layer 1 (z-0) — refractive glass surface** — inline-styled to match `Header.tsx:193–196` exactly: `backdropFilter: 'blur(2px)' + filter: 'url(#buybox-glass-distortion)' + isolation: 'isolate'`. This is the layer that produces the "shader blur" — the displacement map distorting the source under specular lighting.

3. **Layer 2 (z-1) — milky tint** — `Header.tsx:198–207` exactly: `backdropFilter: 'blur(2px)' + backgroundColor: 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25)'` (back to the header's true 25% — no more 45% hack).

4. **Outer .buyBox** — reverted to header parity: `transparent` background, `backdrop-filter: blur(12px)`, `1.5px solid rgba(229, 232, 235, 0.5)` border, `0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.1), 0 6px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.05)` shadow, `border-radius: 18px`, `overflow: hidden`. No saturate boost, no gradient — exactly the header.

5. **Removed the `.buyBoxBackdrop` / `.buyBoxTint` CSS module classes** since the inner layers are now inline-styled (eliminates any CSS module compilation variability).

If after a hard refresh it still looks different from the header, the visual delta is purely from what's painting **behind** each pill — the header floats over the gallery hero (rich content → blur and tint show clearly), the buyBox sits in a column over mostly white (same recipe → less visible result). That's a backdrop-content issue, not a recipe issue. The shader/blur recipe itself is now provably identical.
